q: Yesterday, did you say anything you wished you hadn't? If so, can you name what was driving it — fear, irritation, the wish to be liked?
Was there a moment today when you almost took something — attention, time, energy — that wasn't freely offered? What stopped you, or what didn't?
Did you keep your word today, in the small things as well as the large?
Notice your speech today: was any of it sharp, deceptive, idle, or divisive? No judgment — just see clearly.
Was there an action today you would not want a wise friend to know about? If so, what does that tell you?
Which hindrance was strongest in your sit today: sensual desire, ill will, sloth, restlessness, or doubt? Name it without trying to fix it.
When restlessness arose today, did you stay with it or run from it? What does running look like for you specifically?
Did doubt visit you today — about the practice, about yourself, about whether any of this matters? How did you meet it?
Was there sloth or torpor in your sit? Can you tell the difference between needing rest and avoiding the seat?
Notice ill will today — the small irritations, the silent judgments. Where did they land most often?
Was there one moment today when you noticed an unwholesome state arising and chose not to follow it? Even a small one counts.
Did you abandon a familiar mental pattern that was already running, even briefly?
Was there a wholesome quality you tried to develop today that wasn't already strong in you? What was it?
What is one wholesome state in you that you maintained today even though it wanted to fade?
Did you wish well to anyone today who you find difficult? Not in meditation — in actual interaction.
Did you celebrate someone else's good fortune today, fully and without comparison?
When you encountered someone suffering today, was there compassion — or did you turn away?
Was there equanimity in you today, especially around things you cannot control?
Who in your life right now most needs your kindness — and have you offered it?
Were you present for any meal today? Or did all of them happen on autopilot?
Notice your body right now. What is it telling you that you have been ignoring?
Was there a moment today when you caught yourself caught — and let go?
How many times today did you reach for your phone without intention? No shame. Just the count.
What did you do today that you cannot remember doing?
Today you used the adze. You cannot see what wore down. That is not your business. Did you use it?
Beyond the cushion: was there one act of generosity today, however small?
Did your practice today touch anyone other than yourself? It is allowed to.
Of the eight factors of the path — view, intention, speech, action, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, concentration — which one most needs your attention this week?
If today were the only day of practice you ever had, would you have walked it the same way?
